extractions.text: Then the reply from Portland: MINE TOO. WE ARE WORKING ON THE CELESTIAL CURRENT ALONE.
extractions.reasoning: A fictional conversation and specific telegraph message presented as canon within the series.

extractions.text: The conversation is famous. Reproduced in textbooks. Cited as evidence that the Carrington Event was merely a curiosity???a moment when nature demonstrated its power and men responded with wonder instead of fear.
extractions.reasoning: This reinterprets a real historical event (Carrington Event) within the series' fictional context, defining how it's understood in this world's 'textbooks'.

extractions.text: Carrington Event
extractions.reasoning: The user explicitly stated that the Carrington Event is an independently verifiable historical fact.

extractions.text: Decades later, when digital imaging allowed the damaged paper to be examined without handling, a pattern emerged. A researcher ran the interference sections through pattern recognition software. The noise wasn't noise.
extractions.reasoning: The discovery of a hidden pattern in what was previously dismissed as noise, thanks to new technology.

extractions.text: The pattern repeats throughout the entire two-hour transmission. Hidden in the margins. Buried in the gaps between words. A second conversation running beneath the first???encoded, patient, waiting to be read.
extractions.reasoning: Major reveal that the 'noise' constitutes a persistent, hidden message or 'second conversation'.

extractions.text: The sky was speaking. The wire was taking dictation.
extractions.reasoning: Personification of natural phenomena (sky) and technology (wire) to convey the profound nature of the message.

extractions.text: And there is one final anomaly. At twelve-forty-two in the afternoon, a message arrived from the direction of Portland. It is not in English. It is not in standard Morse code. It is a continuous stream of dots. No dashes. Just dots.
extractions.reasoning: Discovery of a second, distinct anomalous message type.

extractions.text: In two thousand nineteen, a sound engineer converted the dots to audio. She treated the spacing as a waveform???each dot a pulse, each gap a trough. The result was not static. It was a tone. A pure, sustained sine wave. Frequency: fourteen kilohertz.
extractions.reasoning: Modern interpretation of the dot stream, revealing its true nature as a specific audio frequency.

extractions.text: George Bliss survived the Carrington Event. He continued working at the Boston telegraph office for two more years. Then, in eighteen sixty-one, he resigned. He cited nervous exhaustion. He moved to a farm in Vermont. When the electrical grid arrived decades later, he refused to connect his house.
extractions.reasoning: Detailed biography and actions of George Bliss, crucial to his character arc and the impact of the Carrington Event within the series' fiction.

extractions.text: He cited nervous exhaustion.

extractions.text: Fourteen kilohertz. The exact frequency of the hum Dane Tesla heard in the cellar beneath his father's church. The exact frequency of the vibration he felt in his fingertips when he pushed his hand into the beam of white light.
extractions.reasoning: Connects the specific 14 kHz frequency to Dane Tesla's past, mysterious experiences.
